2605.19111.title: FAGER: Factually Grounded Evaluation and Refinement of Text-to-Image Models
2605.19111.paper_summary: Existing text-to-image (T2I) evaluation metrics mainly assess whether generated images align with information explicitly stated in the prompt, but often fail to capture factual requirements that are implicit, externally grounded, or identity-defining. As a result, they are not well suited for evaluating factual correctness in prompts involving scientific knowledge, historical facts, products, or culture-specific concepts. We propose FActually Grounded Evaluation and Refinement (FAGER), an agentic framework that evaluates whether generated images correctly reflect visually verifiable facts grounded in or implied by the prompt, while also providing actionable feedback for improvement. FAGER first constructs a structured factual rubric by combining LLM-based fact proposal with reference-guided visual fact extraction and verification, then converts the rubric into question-answer pairs for VLM-based evaluation. To validate FAGER as a factuality metric, we introduce a Factual A/B test, which measures whether a metric prefers factual reference images over corresponding generated images. Across five datasets spanning science, history, products, culture, and knowledge-intensive concepts, FAGER consistently outperforms prior metrics on this test. We further show that FAGER can be used to refine T2I outputs in a fully training-free manner, yielding substantial factuality gains across datasets.
2605.19111.paper_summary_zh: 現有的文本到圖像 (T2I) 評估指標主要評估生成的圖像是否與提示中明確陳述的信息一致，但往往無法捕捉隱含的、外部基礎的或身份定義的事實要求。因此，它們不太適合評估涉及科學知識、歷史事實、產品或文化特定概念的提示中的事實正確性。我們提出了實際基礎評估與改進 (FAGER)，這是一個代理框架，用於評估生成的圖像是否正確反映基於提示的可視驗證事實，並提供可行的改進反饋。FAGER 首先通過結合基於 LLM 的事實提議與參考引導的視覺事實提取和驗證來構建結構化的事實標準，然後將該標準轉換為問題-答案對以進行基於 VLM 的評估。為了驗證 FAGER 作為事實性指標的有效性，我們引入了一個事實 A/B 測試，該測試測量指標是否偏好事實參考圖像而非相應的生成圖像。在涵蓋科學、歷史、產品、文化和知識密集型概念的五個數據集上，FAGER 在這一測試中始終優於之前的指標。我們進一步表明，FAGER 可以以完全無需訓練的方式來改進 T2I 輸出，並在各數據集上實現顯著的事實性提升。
2605.19111.author: Youngsun Lim et.al.
2605.19111.authors: Youngsun Lim, Cusuh Ham, Pin-Yu Chen, Deepti Ghadiyaram
